summary: Matched the Home-Run Contest stage function grHomeRun_8021ED74 at 100%. The placeholder was replaced with a small void function that initializes a BobOmbRain item-spawn descriptor, obtains a stage JObj via Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F), extracts a position with lb_8000B1CC, and calls it_8026BE84. The header prototype was tightened from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void(void), and grhomerun.c gained the it/it_26B1.h include needed for BobOmbRain and the item spawn function.
what_changed: Replaced the grHomeRun_8021ED74 placeholder comment with a concrete implementation.
Declared and initialized a BobOmbRain local with x0 and x4 set to NULL, x14 set to 0xB, x18 set to 0, and x1C.b0 set to 1.
Fetched an HSD_JObj pointer using Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F).
Called lb_8000B1CC(jobj, NULL, &bobomb_rain.x8_vec) to fill the BobOmbRain position vector.
Called it_8026BE84(&bobomb_rain), tying the stage function to Bob-omb rain item creation.
Updated grhomerun.h prototype for grHomeRun_8021ED74 from UNK_RET grHomeRun_8021ED74(UNK_PARAMS) to void grHomeRun_8021ED74(void).
smart_moves: Used the specific item header it/it_26B1.h instead of leaving item types and functions as unknowns, allowing the stage code to express the BobOmbRain data path directly.
Converted the header declaration to an exact void(void) signature once the function body showed no inputs and no return value.
Kept the implementation compact and data-oriented: initialize the item descriptor, derive its vector from a stage JObj, then pass it to the item subsystem.
Preserved likely matching-sensitive initialization order, including separate assignments to BobOmbRain fields before the lb_8000B1CC and it_8026BE84 calls.
decomp_lessons: Small stage functions may primarily act as bridges between ground/JObj lookup helpers and item-spawn helpers; matching them can require importing the relevant item header rather than treating calls as opaque.
When a decompiled body proves a function has no parameters and no return, update the header from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS immediately so downstream work benefits from the stronger signature.
For item descriptor structs, field-by-field initialization with exact constants can be important for matching and for documenting semantics even when many field names remain partially generic.
Stage object IDs such as 0x7F can be used to locate a JObj whose transform feeds a spawn vector via lb_8000B1CC.
naming_conventions: The PR retained the existing stage prefix and address-based name grHomeRun_8021ED74.
The local variable was named bobomb_rain, matching the BobOmbRain type and making the item-spawn intent clearer.
The HSD_JObj local was named jobj, consistent with common decomp shorthand for joint object pointers.
The item type name BobOmbRain uses the project-visible casing from it/it_26B1.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched grHomeRun_8021ED74 by using a stack-allocated BobOmbRain struct and assigning its fields individually.
Used NULL assignments for pointer fields x0 and x4 rather than alternate zeroing constructs.
Set bobomb_rain.x1C.b0 = 1 as a bitfield-style assignment, preserving the struct access form visible in the matched C.
Called lb_8000B1CC with NULL as the middle argument and &bobomb_rain.x8_vec as the output vector.
Verification reported ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt reports the summary and verification; no reviewer rationale or requested changes are available.
follow_up_queries: Find other grhomerun functions that spawn items through BobOmbRain or it_8026BE84.
Search for other uses of Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F) to confirm the stage object role.
Inspect it/it_26B1.h for BobOmbRain field names that could be improved beyond x0, x4, x14, x18, x1C, and x8_vec.
Look for similar stage functions that still have UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS but are likely void(void) item-spawn helpers.
